Scenarios

What Changed

▼

Generated from git diff by delegated diff-reading agent. Code diffs are ground truth.

Infrastructure:

Three Python scripts (persist_decisions.py, scaffold_review_pack_data.py, create_postmerge_issues.py) replaced hardcoded joeyfezster/building_ai_w_ai repo slug with auto-detection from git remote get-url origin, with --repo CLI override. SSH URL parsing uses SCP-style detection (no scheme prefix) rather than the original @-based check. The publish-subtrees.yml workflow gained a three-rule path rewriting pipeline (self-references, cross-references, skill paths) plus a verification gate that fails the publish if stale monorepo paths remain. CI paths-ignore added to skip builds for doc-only changes.

Product:

No product code changes. All changes target factory infrastructure, CI workflows, package documentation, and skill definitions.

Factory

CI workflow (ci.yaml) gained paths-ignore for docs, markdown, review-prompts, and review-pack references/assets. The publish-subtrees.yml workflow was substantially expanded with path rewriting steps (Rule 1: strip self-prefix, Rule 2: cross-reference URLs, Rule 2b: skill install paths) and a verification gate per package. create_postmerge_issues.py got the same repo-slug auto-detection as the other scripts.

FileAgentsZoneNotable
packages/dark-factory/scripts/persist_decisions.py CHA SEA ADB+ TIN/A factory _get_repo_slug() is copy-pasted across 3 files — maintenance drift risk
CHAcode-health

The hardcoded REPO_SLUG = "joeyfezster/building_ai_w_ai" constant is replaced by a _get_repo_slug() function that auto-detects from git remote get-url origin with a --repo CLI override. The function correctly handles both HTTPS and SCP-style SSH URLs. The get_pr_url() and build_persisted_decision() signatures are updated to thread the slug through. Clean, minimal change with correct fallback chain.

SEAsecurity

All subprocess calls use list-form arguments (no shell=True). The _get_repo_slug() function parses URL output from git remote get-url origin which is a local-only operation. No user-controlled input reaches shell commands.

ADB+adversarial

Evidence: The exact same _get_repo_slug() function appears in persist_decisions.py, scaffold_review_pack_data.py, and create_postmerge_issues.py. All three are byte-identical implementations.
Impact: A future bug fix (e.g., handling a new URL format) must be applied to all three files independently. This is a known trade-off — the plan document (docs/plan_standalone_packages.md) explicitly defers shared module extraction to issue #23.
Mitigation: Tracked as out-of-scope. Acceptable for this PR.

TIN/Atest-integrity

The _get_repo_slug() addition and get_pr_url() / build_persisted_decision() signature changes are backward-compatible (new parameters have defaults). No tests are added for the new function, but this is infrastructure tooling where the publish workflow's verification gate serves as the integration test.
packages/pr-review-pack/scripts/scaffold_review_pack_data.py CHA CHB+ factory GraphQL owner/name split lacks defensive handling for malformed slugs
CHAcode-health

The scaffold script received the same _get_repo_slug() function and --repo CLI arg. The repo slug is now threaded through build_header() and the GraphQL comment query. The owner/name split for GraphQL (owner, name = repo_slug.split("/")) handles the happy path correctly; empty strings when no slash is present would produce a failed GraphQL query but not a crash.

CHB+code-health

Evidence: Line 442: owner, name = repo_slug.split("/") if "/" in repo_slug else ("", ""). If _get_repo_slug() returns a slug with more than one / (e.g., from an unusual remote URL), this would raise a ValueError on unpacking.
Impact: Low — _get_repo_slug() uses split(":")[-1] or split("/")[-2:] which reliably produce owner/repo for standard GitHub URLs. Edge case only.
Mitigation: Could use repo_slug.split("/", 1) for robustness, but current implementation works for all standard GitHub remote formats.
scripts/create_postmerge_issues.py CHA SEA factory Same _get_repo_slug() pattern — consistent across all three scripts
CHAcode-health

Identical auto-detection function applied. The script previously had no explicit slug handling (relied on --repo arg only). Now auto-detects with override, matching the other two scripts.

SEAsecurity

The _get_repo_slug() function uses the same safe pattern. The gh issue create call passes all arguments as list elements, avoiding shell injection. The --repo argument feeds directly into the gh CLI's --repo flag.
.github/workflows/ci.yaml SEB+ TIN/A factory paths-ignore could skip CI for changes to factory scripts accessed via .claude/skills/ symlinks
SEB+security

Evidence: The paths-ignore list includes .claude/skills/pr-review-pack/references/** and .claude/skills/pr-review-pack/assets/**. These are symlink targets — changes in the actual packages/ directories would still trigger CI because the paths-ignore matches the symlink path, not the actual file path.
Impact: Minimal. The paths-ignore is correctly scoped: docs/**, *.md, packages/review-prompts/**, and the pr-review-pack reference/asset paths are all non-code artifacts that cannot affect product validation or factory-self-test results.
Note: The factory-self-test job is not excluded from path filtering — it triggers on the same events. If a docs-only commit also touched a scenario format, CI would still not run. However, the paths-ignore patterns are conservative (only docs and review-pack non-code assets) and this risk is low.

TIN/Atest-integrity
CI workflow YAML configuration. Test integrity review is not applicable to workflow files.
.github/workflows/publish-subtrees.yml CHA ADB+ SEA TIN/A factory Verification grep uses -e flags per pattern instead of alternation — correct but verbose
CHAcode-health

The path rewriting follows the plan exactly: Rule 1 (strip self-prefix) handles all current and future self-references. Rule 2 (cross-references to standalone URLs) handles the stable set of sibling packages. Rule 2b (skill install paths) strips .claude/skills/{name}/ prefixes. The verification gate (grep -rn for stale patterns) catches anything the rules miss and fails the publish job with an actionable error message. This is a clean open/closed design — new files are handled automatically, new cross-reference patterns are caught by the gate.

ADB+adversarial

Evidence: The verification step uses separate -e flags for each pattern (-e "packages/dark-factory", -e "packages/pr-review-pack", etc.) rather than the plan's alternation syntax (packages/dark-factory\|packages/pr-review-pack). The plan document (line 164-167) shows backslash-escaped alternation, but the implementation uses explicit -e flags.
Impact: Both approaches are functionally equivalent. The -e flag approach is actually more portable across grep implementations. The pattern scope is package-specific — each package's verification only greps for patterns relevant to that package plus the shared packages/review-prompts pattern. This is more precise than the plan's catch-all approach.

SEAsecurity

SSH deploy keys are written to ~/.ssh/deploy_key with chmod 600, used via GIT_SSH_COMMAND environment variable, and cleaned up in an if: always() step. The ssh-keyscan call prevents MITM but the known_hosts file is ephemeral (CI runner). No secrets are logged or exposed in output.

TIN/Atest-integrity
Publish workflow YAML configuration. Test integrity review is not applicable to workflow files.
packages/dark-factory/SKILL.md CHB ADB+ CHA factory Duplicate entries in Reference Files section — dark_factory.md and factory_architecture.html listed twice
CHBcode-health

Evidence: Lines 325-329 of the updated SKILL.md list docs/dark_factory.md and docs/factory_architecture.html each twice — once without a description suffix and once with. The first pair says "factory documentation" and "factory architecture"; the second pair says "factory documentation" and "factory architecture diagram".
Impact: Cosmetic — no functional impact on the skill's operation. Agents reading the reference list see redundant entries, which is slightly confusing but not harmful.
Fix: Remove the duplicate pair (lines 328-329).

ADB+adversarial

Evidence: Line 330: packages/pr-review-pack/SKILL.md — PR review pack skill (Step 12). In the monorepo this path is valid. After subtree split and path rewriting (Rule 2), it becomes https://github.com/joeyfezster/pr-review-pack/tree/main/SKILL.md which is correct for standalone consumption.
Impact: None in standalone context — the publish workflow's Rule 2 rewrites this correctly. In monorepo context the path is valid as-is. No issue.

CHAcode-health

The Prerequisites section previously listed Repository: joeyfezster/building_ai_w_ai and Chrome is logged into Codex. The repo reference is removed (now auto-detected). The Chrome prerequisite is moved to the README's Permissions & Setup section where it belongs (operational setup, not skill prerequisites). The Reference Files section is reformatted from a verbose key-value list to a concise bullet list.
packages/pr-review-pack/SKILL.md CHA ADA factory Prerequisites moved to reference doc — SKILL.md stays concise, content preserved
CHAcode-health

The full prerequisite gate-checking procedure (53 lines including GraphQL queries, comment handling protocol, and routing rules) was extracted to references/prerequisites.md. The SKILL.md now has a one-liner pointing to the reference doc. The content in references/prerequisites.md is byte-identical to what was removed from SKILL.md. The Quick Start section (37 lines) was also removed — it duplicated the Three-Pass Pipeline section. The allowed-tools frontmatter was added to pre-approve bash patterns for the multi-step pipeline.

ADAadversarial

Cross-referenced the removed SKILL.md content against references/prerequisites.md (gate-checking procedure), references/pass2b-invocation.md (agent invocation), and references/pass2b-output-schema.md (JSON output shapes). All substantive content is preserved. The removed Quick Start section was redundant with the Three-Pass Pipeline section that remains.
packages/dark-factory/README.md CHA Comprehensive Prerequisites and Permissions sections added

README now documents all required dependencies (Python 3.12+, git, gh CLI, Claude Code with Agent Teams, OpenAI Codex) and optional gate tools (ruff, radon, vulture, bandit). Installation section with clone commands for both dark-factory and pr-review-pack. Permissions & Setup section covering Chrome/Codex browser setup and Claude Code allowed-tools. Permissions & Setup section covers allowed-tools, Claude Code settings, and macOS screen recording/accessibility grants for visual validation. These were previously missing or incomplete.
packages/pr-review-pack/requirements.txt SEA Single dependency file for pyyaml — the only third-party Python requirement
SEAsecurity

New file with pyyaml>=6.0. This is the only third-party Python dependency in the pr-review-pack package (used by scaffold_review_pack_data.py for zone registry parsing). The version constraint >=6.0 is appropriate — pyyaml 6.0+ uses the C loader by default, which is both faster and more secure.

CHAcode-health

Clear status definitions (implemented, wip, abandoned, deferred) and rules for frontmatter maintenance. Explains the relationship between plan frontmatter and the factory decision log — they track different things and complement each other.
docs/plan_standalone_packages.md ADA Thorough plan document with dependency audit, work streams, and verification steps
ADAadversarial

367-line plan document covering the full scope of this PR: dependency audit for both packages, four work streams (auto-detect repo slug, publish workflow rewriting, dependency docs, end-to-end verification), and an explicit out-of-scope section linking to tracked issues. Uses the new frontmatter convention (status: wip, pr: 26). The plan's SSH URL parsing suggestion (":" in url and "@" in url) was improved in implementation to ":" in url and not url.startswith(("https://", "http://", "ssh://")) — a better approach that catches SCP-style URLs without scheme prefixes.

Key Decisions

▼
1
Auto-detect repo slug from git remote instead of hardcoding
Standalone package users in their own repos need scripts that work without editing source code

Three scripts hardcoded joeyfezster/building_ai_w_ai as the GitHub repo slug. This was replaced with a _get_repo_slug() function that auto-detects from git remote get-url origin, with a --repo owner/repo CLI override for non-standard setups.

The SSH URL parsing was improved from the plan's original approach (":" in url and "@" in url) to a more robust check: ":" in url and not url.startswith(("https://", "http://", "ssh://")). This correctly identifies SCP-style URLs (e.g., git@github.com:owner/repo.git) by detecting the absence of a scheme prefix, rather than checking for @ which can appear in HTTPS URLs with credentials.

The function is copy-pasted across all three scripts — shared module extraction is deferred to issue #23.

factory
FileChange
packages/dark-factory/scripts/persist_decisions.pyReplaced REPO_SLUG constant with _get_repo_slug(), added --repo CLI arg
packages/pr-review-pack/scripts/scaffold_review_pack_data.pyAdded _get_repo_slug(), --repo CLI arg, threaded slug through build_header and GraphQL query
scripts/create_postmerge_issues.pyAdded _get_repo_slug(), --repo CLI arg replacing implicit repo handling
2
Open/closed publish workflow with verification gate
Monorepo paths must be rewritten for standalone repos; a brittle sed list would break on every new file

The publish-subtrees.yml workflow gained a three-rule path rewriting pipeline that runs before subtree split, plus a verification gate that fails the publish if stale monorepo paths survive rewriting.

Rule 1 (self-references): A single sed per package strips the package's own monorepo prefix (packages/dark-factory/ or packages/pr-review-pack/). This automatically handles all current and future self-referencing paths — no maintenance needed when adding new files.

Rule 2 (cross-references): Replaces references to sibling packages with standalone GitHub repo URLs. packages/review-prompts/ maps to the local review-prompts/ directory (already resolved from symlink in the previous step).

Rule 2b (skill paths): Strips .claude/skills/{name}/ prefixes so monorepo skill-install paths become local-root paths in standalone context.

Verification gate: grep -rn for stale patterns (packages/*, .claude/skills/{name}/) across all .md, .py, .yaml, .yml files. Fails the publish with an actionable error if anything remains. CONTRIBUTING.md is excluded (may legitimately reference the monorepo).

factory
FileChange
.github/workflows/publish-subtrees.ymlAdded 3 new steps per package: rewrite paths, verify no stale paths, commit transforms
3
CI paths-ignore for doc-only changes
Documentation and review-pack reference changes should not trigger CI builds that test product code

Added paths-ignore to both push and pull_request triggers in ci.yaml. Ignored paths: docs/**, *.md, **/*.md, packages/review-prompts/**, .claude/skills/pr-review-pack/references/**, .claude/skills/pr-review-pack/assets/**.

These paths contain documentation, review pack templates, agent reference docs, and review prompts — none of which affect product code validation or factory self-tests. The factory-self-test job is included in the same workflow, so it also skips on doc-only changes. This is acceptable because factory self-tests check scenario format and script linting, not documentation content.

factory
FileChange
.github/workflows/ci.yamlAdded paths-ignore for docs, markdown, review-prompts, and review-pack reference/asset paths
4
SKILL.md audit: move verbose content to reference docs, add allowed-tools
SKILL.md was too long for agent context windows; move reference material to dedicated files

The pr-review-pack SKILL.md was reduced from ~375 lines to ~283 lines by extracting two sections to reference docs:

  • Prerequisites gate-checking procedure (53 lines including GraphQL queries and comment routing) moved to references/prerequisites.md
  • Quick Start section (37 lines) removed — it duplicated the Three-Pass Pipeline section that remains

Two new reference docs were added: pass2b-invocation.md (394 lines, exact agent spawning patterns for Pass 2b) and pass2b-output-schema.md (486 lines, JSON shapes with types, examples, and common-mistake warnings).

The dark-factory SKILL.md was trimmed: removed hardcoded repo reference and Chrome prerequisite from Prerequisites, reformatted Reference Files from verbose key-value format to concise bullet list.

The pr-review-pack SKILL.md also gained allowed-tools in its frontmatter to pre-approve bash patterns, matching the dark-factory SKILL.md pattern.

factory
FileChange
packages/pr-review-pack/SKILL.mdExtracted prerequisites to reference doc, removed duplicate Quick Start, added allowed-tools
packages/dark-factory/SKILL.mdRemoved hardcoded repo and Chrome prereqs, reformatted Reference Files list

packages/pr-review-pack/references/pass2b-output-schema.mdNew: JSON output shapes with TypeScript types, examples, and anti-patterns
packages/pr-review-pack/references/prerequisites.mdNew: gate-checking procedure extracted from SKILL.md
5
Complete README documentation for standalone package consumers
Both packages were missing prerequisites, installation instructions, and permissions setup

Both package READMEs were updated to be standalone-consumable:

dark-factory README: Added complete Prerequisites section (required: Python, git, gh CLI, Claude Code, Codex; optional: ruff, radon, vulture, bandit with graceful degradation note). Added Installation section with clone commands for both dark-factory and its pr-review-pack dependency. Added Permissions & Setup section covering Chrome/Codex browser setup and Claude Code allowed-tools.

pr-review-pack README: Added Prerequisites section (Python 3.12+ with pyyaml, git, gh CLI, Claude Code). Updated Quick Start with clone + pip install + run instructions. Added Permissions & Setup section covering allowed-tools, Claude Code settings.json overrides, and macOS screen recording/accessibility grants for visual validation.

A requirements.txt file was added for pr-review-pack with pyyaml>=6.0 — the only third-party Python dependency.

factory
FileChange
packages/dark-factory/README.mdAdded Prerequisites, Installation, and Permissions & Setup sections
packages/pr-review-pack/README.mdAdded Prerequisites, Quick Start, and Permissions & Setup sections
packages/pr-review-pack/requirements.txtNew: pyyaml>=6.0 dependency file
6
Decision Record Convention for plan documents
Plan documents lacked lifecycle metadata — no way to tell if a plan was executed, deferred, or abandoned

Established a YAML frontmatter convention for plan documents in docs/CONVENTIONS.md. The schema includes type, status (implemented/wip/abandoned/deferred), pr number, created/updated dates, and optional supersedes field.

The convention distinguishes plan frontmatter (lifecycle of a plan document) from the factory decision log (individual decisions during PR review). Both systems complement each other. The plan document for this PR (docs/plan_standalone_packages.md) uses the new convention with status: wip and pr: 26.

factory

docs/CONVENTIONS.mdNew: Decision Record Convention with YAML frontmatter schema
docs/plan_standalone_packages.mdNew: plan document for standalone package fixes, uses new frontmatter convention

Post-Merge Items

▼
LOW Extract _get_repo_slug() to shared utility module

The same _get_repo_slug() function is copy-pasted across three scripts: persist_decisions.py, scaffold_review_pack_data.py, and create_postmerge_issues.py. All three are byte-identical. This is an intentional trade-off for this PR (shared module extraction is tracked as issue #23), but should be resolved before the next script that needs repo slug detection.

## packages/dark-factory/scripts/persist_decisions.py, lines 36-48 def _get_repo_slug(override: str | None = None) -> str: """Return owner/repo from CLI flag or git remote origin.""" if override: return override url = subprocess.check_output( ["git", "remote", "get-url", "origin"], text=True ).strip() # SCP-style (git@host:owner/repo.git) has no scheme prefix if ":" in url and not url.startswith(("https://", "http://", "ssh://")): slug = url.split(":")[-1] else: slug = "/".join(url.split("/")[-2:]) return slug.removesuffix(".git")
Failure scenario
A bug fix to _get_repo_slug() (e.g., handling GitLab or Bitbucket URL formats) must be applied to 3 files independently. One gets missed, causing a runtime failure in that script.
Resolution
Shared _utils.py module with a single _get_repo_slug() definition. All 3 scripts import from it. Issue #23 resolved.
factory
LOW Remove duplicate Reference Files entries in dark-factory SKILL.md

The Reference Files section in packages/dark-factory/SKILL.md lists docs/dark_factory.md and docs/factory_architecture.html each twice (lines 325-326 and 328-329). This appears to be a copy-paste artifact from the reformatting.

## packages/dark-factory/SKILL.md, lines 324-331 - `docs/code_quality_standards.md` — code quality standards - `docs/dark_factory.md` — factory documentation - `docs/factory_architecture.html` — factory architecture - `docs/decisions/decision_log.json` — cumulative decision archive - `docs/dark_factory.md` — factory documentation - `docs/factory_architecture.html` — factory architecture diagram - `packages/pr-review-pack/SKILL.md` — PR review pack skill (Step 12) - `specs/*.md` — component specifications
Failure scenario
An agent reading the Reference Files section sees redundant entries and may waste context tokens processing duplicates. Minor confusion, no functional impact.
Resolution
Duplicate entries removed. Each reference file appears exactly once in the list.
factory
COSMETIC Update plan document status to implemented after merge

The plan document docs/plan_standalone_packages.md has status: wip in its frontmatter. After this PR merges, the status should be updated to implemented per the Decision Record Convention in docs/CONVENTIONS.md.

## docs/plan_standalone_packages.md, lines 1-7 --- type: plan status: wip pr: 26 created: 2025-03-05 updated: 2025-03-07 ---
Failure scenario
The plan document remains in 'wip' status indefinitely, making it unclear whether the plan was executed. Future readers must cross-reference PR #26 to determine status.
Resolution
Frontmatter updated to status: implemented and updated date set to merge date.
factory
MEDIUM Zone registry gap: packages/ paths not mapped to any zone

The zone registry (.claude/zone-registry.yaml) maps scripts/**, .github/**, and .claude/skills/** to the "factory" zone, but packages/** is not mapped. Of the 17 files in this PR, 14 are under packages/ or docs/ and match no zone. This means the architecture diagram in the review pack cannot highlight which zones these changes touch, and decision-to-zone verification cannot confirm zone claims for most files.

## .claude/zone-registry.yaml, lines 51-55 factory: paths: ["scripts/**", ".github/**", ".claude/skills/**", "docs/dark_factory.md", "docs/code_quality_standards.md", "CLAUDE.md"] specs: [] category: factory label: "Factory"
Failure scenario
Future review packs for PRs touching packages/ show most files as unmapped. Architecture diagrams are incomplete. Decision verification produces false 'unverified' flags. This is already tracked as issue #21.
Resolution
Zone registry updated with patterns for packages/dark-factory/**, packages/pr-review-pack/**, packages/review-prompts/**, and docs/**. Architecture diagram accurately reflects factory infrastructure changes.
factory
